title: Today's Tech Brief: The people who actually want AI to replace humanity
summary: A look at AI successionism, Cloudflare's new verification method, and more.
imageAlt: Image from source article for The people who actually want AI to replace humanity
imageCaption: The people who actually want AI to replace humanity
imageSourceTitle: The people who actually want AI to replace humanity

body.text: The Vox article, written by Sigal Samuel, explores this idea and its implications. It seems that some people, known as 'AI successionists,' believe that AI has the potential to surpass human intelligence and take over. This raises interesting questions about the future of humanity and our role in the world. Meanwhile, Cloudflare has updated its 'Verify you're human' device verification, also known as Turnstile. The new method requires fingerprintable WebGL, which has raised some eyebrows. According to a report on hacktivis.me, this change has sparked concerns about user privacy and security.

Another news item that caught my attention is the ongoing debate about the ethics of AI development. As AI becomes more advanced, we're faced with tough questions about its potential impact on society. While AI successionism might be a fringe movement, it highlights the need for a broader conversation about the role of AI in our lives.

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
